reasoning: With only 10 days remaining in 2025, the likelihood of Apple announcing a $500M+ AI acquisition is exceedingly low. The tech industry heavily slows down during the late December holiday period, making major M&A announcements rare. Furthermore, Apple's historical M&A strategy favors smaller "acqui-hires" rather than mega-deals. While there were rumors in August 2025 about internal debates over acquiring Mistral or Perplexity, subsequent reports indicated Apple told bankers it was sticking to smaller deals, and no tangible acquisition developments have leaked since. In early 2025, Apple also committed to a $500 billion multi-year investment in US-based AI infrastructure and internal R&D, underscoring its preference to build rather than buy large entities. The Manifold market had already priced this at just 6% back on December 11, and the passage of time without any impending rumors strongly pushes the odds to the absolute minimum.
